exercises.title: SIGNAL GRADE
exercises.prompt:
Define **signal_grade(strength)** that returns a label based on the number **strength**:
- strength **< 30** -> "WEAK"
- **30 to 69** -> "OK"
- **70 or more** -> "STRONG"

Chain the cases with **if / elseif / else**.
~~~lua
signal_grade(10)  -- "WEAK"
signal_grade(50)  -- "OK"
signal_grade(95)  -- "STRONG"
~~~
exercises.starter:
function signal_grade(strength)
  -- TODO: branch on strength
  return ""
end
exercises.solution:
function signal_grade(strength)
  if strength < 30 then
    return "WEAK"
  elseif strength < 70 then
    return "OK"
  else
    return "STRONG"
  end
end
exercises.hint: if strength < 30 then ... elseif strength < 70 then ... else ... end
exercises.lore: The tower reads the wire and renders its verdict.

exercises.title: VOLTAGE CLAMP
exercises.brief: Pin a value inside a range.
exercises.prompt:
Define **clamp(value, low, high)** that pulls **value** back inside the range:
- if value is **below low**, return **low**
- if value is **above high**, return **high**
- otherwise return **value** unchanged

Use **if / elseif / else**.
~~~lua
clamp(5, 0, 10)   -- 5
clamp(-3, 0, 10)  -- 0
clamp(99, 0, 10)  -- 10
~~~
exercises.starter:
function clamp(value, low, high)
  -- TODO: clamp value between low and high
  return value
end
exercises.solution:
function clamp(value, low, high)
  if value < low then
    return low
  elseif value > high then
    return high
  else
    return value
  end
end
exercises.hint: if value < low then return low elseif value > high then return high else return value end
exercises.lore: No surge escapes the regulator alive.

exercises.title: PEAK CELL
exercises.prompt:
Define **max_cell(cells)** where **cells** is a non-empty array of numbers. Return the largest value. Start your best guess at `cells[1]`, then walk the array with **ipairs** and keep the bigger value whenever you find one.
~~~lua
max_cell({3, 9, 4})    -- 9
max_cell({7})          -- 7
~~~
exercises.starter:
function max_cell(cells)
  local best = cells[1]
  -- TODO: scan with ipairs and keep the largest
  return 0
end
exercises.solution:
function max_cell(cells)
  local best = cells[1]
  for _, v in ipairs(cells) do
    if v > best then
      best = v
    end
  end
  return best
end
exercises.hint: for _, v in ipairs(cells) do if v > best then best = v end end
exercises.lore: One cell always burns hottest. Find it before it finds you.

exercises.title: OVER THRESHOLD
exercises.prompt:
Define **count_above(readings, limit)** where **readings** is an array of numbers. Return how many entries are **strictly greater than limit**. Walk the array with **ipairs**, keep a counter, and bump it with `count = count + 1` (Lua has no `++`).
~~~lua
count_above({2, 5, 9, 1}, 4)  -- 2   (5 and 9)
count_above({1, 2}, 9)        -- 0
~~~
exercises.starter:
function count_above(readings, limit)
  local count = 0
  -- TODO: count entries strictly greater than limit
  return -1
end
exercises.solution:
function count_above(readings, limit)
  local count = 0
  for _, v in ipairs(readings) do
    if v > limit then
      count = count + 1
    end
  end
  return count
end
exercises.hint: for _, v in ipairs(readings) do if v > limit then count = count + 1 end end
exercises.lore: Every spike past the redline is logged for the tribunal.

exercises.title: DOUBLE WAVE
exercises.brief: Build a new array of doubled values.
exercises.solution:
function double_all(nums)
  local out = {}
  for i = 1, #nums do
    out[i] = nums[i] * 2
  end
  return out
end

tests.name: doubles three
tests.code: local r = double_all({1, 2, 3}); assert(#r == 3 and r[1] == 2 and r[2] == 4 and r[3] == 6)

tests.name: preserves order and length
tests.code: local r = double_all({5, 0, -2}); assert(#r == 3 and r[1] == 10 and r[2] == 0 and r[3] == -4)

exercises.hint: for i = 1, #nums do out[i] = nums[i] * 2 end
exercises.lore: Amplify the waveform, index by index, and ride it out.
